new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a water that complies with Directive 2009/54/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council, characterising it as natural mineral water.

Natural mineral water complying with Directive 2009/54/EC, being a subtype of mineral or aerated water (CN 2201) with no added sugar, sweetening matter or flavouring, presented under CN heading 2201 10.

Natural mineral water complying with Directive 2009/54/EC that is not carbonated (still), classified under CN subheading 2201 10 19. Distinguished from 2201 10 11 (carbonated natural mineral water) by absence of carbonation.

coverage_explanation: The legal text establishes two key discriminating criteria for CN 22011019: (1) the water must be 'natural mineral water' complying with Directive 2009/54/EC, and (2) it must be non-carbonated (still), distinguishing it from sibling 22011011 (carbonated/sparkling natural mineral water). Both criteria are captured: NaturalMineralWaterQuality encodes the Directive 2009/54/EC compliance requirement; the complement restriction on MineralOrAeratedWaterQuality (which represents carbonation/aeration) encodes the non-carbonated criterion. The parent class chain (NaturalMineralWaterNotCarbonated22011019 → NaturalMineralWater220110 → MineralWaterAeratedWater2201) captures the taxonomic placement. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because: (a) the precise regulatory reference to Directive 2009/54/EC cannot be expressed as a formal OWL restriction without a dedicated regulatory-text property, and (b) the complement pattern on MineralOrAeratedWaterQuality is an approximation — ideally a dedicated 'NonCarbonatedQuality' class would be introduced, but that risks inconsistency with the existing 'isCarbonated' data property. The existing 'isCarbonated' data property (eucn:isCarbonated) could alternatively be used via a hasValue restriction (false), but it is not modelled as a quality class under BFO, so the complement approach is used here as the best available approximation within the TBox.
